title: Device Reachability Loss Detection
dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:devicehealth`. from the `devicehealth` modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538) polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/device-health`. Default poll interval: 900s (15m). Volume: ~1 event per managed device per poll. RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `overallHealth` (integer 0-100), `reachabilityHealth` (string), `deviceName` (string), `deviceType` (string), `siteId` (string), `platformId` (string), `managementIpAddress` (string), `softwareVersion` (string).
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:devicehealth" reachabilityHealth="Unreachable" | stats count as unreachable_count earliest(_time) as first_unreachable latest(_time) as last_seen by deviceName, managementIpAddress, deviceType, siteId | eval duration_min=round((last_seen-first_unreachable)/60,0) | sort -duration_min
description: Identifies devices that Catalyst Center can no longer reach, indicating potential hardware failure, network partition, or misconfiguration.
value: Unreachable devices represent the most severe health state — they may be completely down. Rapid detection reduces outage duration and blast radius.
implementation: Requires UC-5.13.3 alerting in place. Filter specifically on `reachabilityHealth="Unreachable"` and schedule frequently (for example every 5–15 minutes) with P1-style routing. Confirm Catalyst Center and Splunk time zones align so `duration_min` matches operator expectations.
visualization: Table of unreachable devices with duration, timeline panel of first to last event, link-out to IP management or CMDB.

references.title: Catalyst Center Device Health API — Cisco DevNet

knownFalsePositives:
**Planned maintenance reload making device temporarily unreachable.** Devices rebooting for firmware upgrades, patch application, or stack changes will show `reachabilityHealth=Unreachable` during the reload (typically 5–20 minutes). Distinguish by correlating with ITSM change records or `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:audit:logs"` for scheduled upgrade activity. Suppress with a `catalyst_maintenance_windows` lookup keyed on deviceName and time range.

**Temporary ACL, firewall rule, or routing change blocking the Catalyst Center management path.** A new ACL or a routing change may block HTTPS (TCP 443) or SNMP from Catalyst Center to the device's `managementIpAddress`. The device continues forwarding user traffic normally but appears unreachable to Catalyst Center. Distinguish by verifying device reachability from an independent management station (ping, SSH). Do not suppress — this is a real management-plane finding, even if the data plane is healthy.

**Network convergence event causing transient ICMP/SNMP probe failure.** During a routing or spanning-tree reconvergence, Catalyst Center's probes may time out for one poll cycle. The device recovers on the next poll. Distinguish by checking whether the `unreachable_count` for the device is exactly 1 and the next poll shows `Reachable`. Suppress by requiring 2+ consecutive polls showing `Unreachable` before raising an alert (30+ minutes of sustained unreachability).

**Catalyst Center SNMP/HTTPS probe timeout on overloaded device.** A device under heavy CPU or memory load may not respond to Catalyst Center's management probes within the timeout window, even though it is functionally operational. Distinguish by correlating with `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:devicehealth"` for `cpuScore<30` or `memoryScore<30` on the same `deviceName`. Do not suppress — the device is genuinely struggling, though not unreachable in the hard-down sense.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• **UC-5.13.3** baselines in place; this UC **narrows** to **`reachabilityHealth="Unreachable"`** only (management-plane loss from Catalyst Center’s view).
• Cisco Catalyst Add-on (7538), **devicehealth** → `cisco:dnac:devicehealth` on `index=catalyst`.
• **Time alignment:** use **same timezone** in Splunk and Catalyst Center operators’ consoles; **`duration_min`** is based on event **`_time`** in the search window.
• See `docs/implementation-guide.md`.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
• **Intent API:** `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/device-health`; **`reachabilityHealth`** is typically **`Reachable`** or **`Unreachable`** (exact strings—**verify in raw** JSON).
• **TA input:** **devicehealth**; poll **every 5–15 minutes** for this use case if you can sustain API load—unreachability is **P1**-class for many teams.
• **Prerequisite:** if **`reachabilityHealth`** is often blank for some platforms, document product-specific behavior and do not **page** on those until fields are reliable.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:devicehealth" reachabilityHealth="Unreachable" | stats count as unreachable_count earliest(_time) as first_unreachable latest(_time) as last_seen by deviceName, managementIpAddress, deviceType, siteId | eval duration_min=round((last_seen-first_unreachable)/60,0) | sort -duration_min
```

Understanding this SPL
• **Implicit filter** on **`reachabilityHealth="Unreachable"`**—if your build uses a different token, **adjust** the literal.
• **`earliest`/`latest`** in the `stats` bracket **unreachable** samples in the chosen window; **`duration_min`** is a **rough** session length, not a full ICMP history.
• **Sort** by **`-duration_min`** emphasizes **sustained** unreachability; pair with a **“first seen”** window filter to suppress **one-poll** blips if needed.

**Pipeline walkthrough**
• **Search-time** filter (or `where`) for **unreachable** rows.
• **Per-device** `stats` for **count** and time span; **`eval`** for minutes.

Step 3 — Validate
• Put a test device in **isolated** management failure (lab) and confirm **Unreachable** in **Catalyst** **Assurance** and the same in Splunk within **two** polls.
• Compare **duration** to **tickets**—if **duration_min** is always **0**, your window may be too **narrow** or only one event exists per device.
• **Join** to **UC-5.13.1** **overallHealth** in a **subsearch** to see if **unreachable** correlates with **null** or **0** health.

Step 4 — Operationalize (alerting)
• **Schedule:** **5–15 min**; **time range** **Last 15–30 min** with overlap to **miss** no polls.
• **Route:** **P1**-style for **production** `siteId`; **throttle** per **device**; **suppression** during **change** **windows** (lookup of **expected** work).
• **Ticket body:** `deviceName`, `managementIpAddress`, `siteId`, **`duration_min`**, **link** to **Device 360** in **Catalyst Center**.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **False unreachability:** **routing** to the **management** **VLAN**; **co-boxed** **firewall** rule; path asymmetry to the controller.
• **Flapping between Reachable/Unreachable:** check **Catalyst** **issues** and **infrastructure** changes before assuming hardware—may be **vPC/HSRP** work or **transient** path loss.
• **User impact unclear:** this UC is **management** **reachability** from **Catalyst Center**—validate **data** plane with **separate** tests if users report outages while the device still “works” in **Assurance** for **data** only.
• **No devicehealth events arriving:** verify the `devicehealth` input is enabled, credentials are valid, and check `index=_internal` for API error or endpoint timeout messages from the TA.
• **NULL or zero values in health fields:** confirm Assurance licensing (DNA Advantage required) and that Assurance analytics is enabled for the site in Catalyst Center.
• **Fewer devices than expected:** check the service account role and virtual domain scope; credential access denied errors in TA logs indicate insufficient permissions.
grandmaExplanation: We catch the moment any device goes completely dark and becomes unreachable, so your team can respond immediately.
